title: Quantitative characterization of the urine and serum metabolomes of children is essential for 'omics' studies.
summary: Commentary on a study by Lau et al. characterizing the serum and urine metabolomes of European children to establish a reference for future exposome studies.
key_findings: Metabolites associated with age, sex, BMI, and dietary intake
Novel association between urinary 4-deoxyerythronic acid and BMI
study_design: Metabolomic profiling of longitudinal birth cohorts
exposure_domain: Exposome (geographical location, socioeconomic status, pollution, diet, gut microflora, metabolism)
limitations: Lack of existing metabolomic reference profiles for children compared to adults
